title: Invert Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, invert the tree (mirror it) and return its root. Every left child becomes the right child and vice versa.
hints: What happens to each node when you invert? Its children swap.
You can do this recursively — invert left subtree, invert right subtree, then swap them.
Base case: null node. For each node, swap left and right children, then recursively invert both.
follow_ups: Can you do it iteratively using a queue?
What's the space complexity in terms of tree height?

title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return its maximum depth. Maximum depth is the number of nodes along the longest path from the root down to the farthest leaf.
hints: The depth of a tree relates to the depth of its subtrees. What's the relationship?
Depth = 1 + max(depth of left subtree, depth of right subtree).
Base case: null returns 0. Recursively get left and right depths, return 1 + max of both.
follow_ups: Can you solve it iteratively using BFS?

title: Validate Binary Search Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, determine if it is a valid binary search tree. In a BST, every node's left subtree contains only values less than the node, and right subtree contains only values greater.
hints: It's not enough to just check if left child < node < right child. Why?
Each node has a valid range it must fall within. The range narrows as you go deeper.
Pass min and max bounds recursively. For left child, upper bound becomes parent's value. For right child, lower bound becomes parent's value.
follow_ups: Can you validate using an inorder traversal?
What if duplicate values are allowed?

title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree
description: Given a binary tree, find the lowest common ancestor (LCA) of two given nodes. The LCA is the deepest node that is an ancestor of both nodes.
hints: If you're at a node and one target is in the left subtree and one is in the right, what does that tell you?
That node must be the LCA! Use recursion to search both subtrees.
Base case: null or node equals p or q. Recurse left and right. If both return non-null, current node is LCA. Otherwise return whichever is non-null.
follow_ups: What if it's a BST instead?
What if you have parent pointers?

title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes' values (left to right, level by level).
hints: Level order means visiting all nodes at depth d before depth d+1. What data structure processes things in order?
A queue processes nodes in FIFO order — perfect for BFS.
Start with root in queue. For each level, process all nodes currently in queue, add their children. Collect each level's values.
follow_ups: Can you do zigzag level order?

title: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
description: Design an algorithm to serialize a binary tree to a string and deserialize that string back to the original tree structure.
hints: You need a way to represent null nodes so you can reconstruct the structure.
Use preorder traversal with a special marker for null nodes.
Serialize: preorder with 'null' for empty nodes, comma-separated. Deserialize: use a queue/iterator, recursively build left then right.
follow_ups: Can you use level order instead?
How would you compress the serialization?
